_note: Bundle covering the Division system (legacy US — Arts 500-503) and the Zone system (IEC-aligned — Arts 505-506).

articles.article_title: Hazardous (Classified) Locations — Classes I, II, III (Division and Zone)
articles.scope: Classification of locations where flammable gases (Class I), combustible dusts (Class II), or ignitible fibers/flyings (Class III) are present. TWO classification systems: Division system (NEC legacy — Class × Division 1/2 × Group A-G) and Zone system (IEC-aligned — Class I × Zone 0/1/2). Article 504 covers intrinsically safe (IS) systems.

key_sections.title: Classifications of Locations
key_sections.summary: Class I (gas/vapor): Division 1 (ignitible concentrations exist under normal conditions) and Division 2 (likely only under abnormal conditions). Class II (dust): Division 1/2 by similar logic. Class III (fibers): Division 1/2.

key_sections.title: Material Groups (Gas/Dust Groups)
key_sections.summary: Class I Groups A-D (Group A: acetylene; B: hydrogen; C: ethylene; D: propane/gasoline). Class II Groups E-G (E: metal dust; F: carbon dust; G: combustible dust like grain). IEC IIA/IIB/IIC equivalents for gases, IIIA/IIIB/IIIC for dusts

key_sections.title: Equipment Marking and Selection
key_sections.summary: Equipment marked for the Class/Division/Group it's listed for. Equipment listed for Zone is generally accepted in Division locations of equivalent severity (with limits)

key_sections.title: Class I Locations (Gas/Vapor)
key_sections.summary: Wiring methods, sealing, equipment for Class I

key_sections.title: Class II Locations (Combustible Dust)
key_sections.summary: Wiring methods and equipment for Class II — dust-ignitionproof or dust-tight

key_sections.title: Class III Locations (Ignitible Fibers/Flyings)
key_sections.summary: Generally less restrictive than I/II — covers textile mills, grain processing

key_sections.title: Intrinsically Safe Systems
key_sections.summary: IS apparatus and associated apparatus per UL 913 / UL 60079-11. Loop calculation for entity parameters (Voc, Isc, Ca, La, Pi, Po, Ci, Li)

key_sections.title: Class I, Zone 0, 1, and 2 Locations (IEC-aligned)
key_sections.summary: Alternative to 501. Zone 0 (continuously present), Zone 1 (occasionally), Zone 2 (only under abnormal conditions). IIA/IIB/IIC gas grouping

key_sections.title: Zone 20, 21, and 22 Locations (Combustible Dust)
key_sections.summary: IEC-aligned dust classification. Zone 20 (dust cloud continuously), 21 (occasionally), 22 (abnormal only). IIIA/IIIB/IIIC dust grouping

tabulated_values.class_division_zone_mapping: see tables_inline + see hazardous-locations-classification.json for full matrix

rows.severity_note: Division 1 is more severe than Zone 1 alone (Division 1 ≈ Zone 0 + 1)

rows._note: NEC Group A is more specific than IEC IIC; IIC covers acetylene + hydrogen + similar
Group B equipment can typically be used in IIC locations and vice versa with caveats

common_errors: Mixing Division-marked equipment and Zone-marked equipment in the same location without confirming the conversion matrix
Treating Class II Group G (grain dust) and Class II Group F (carbon dust) interchangeably — they have different ignition characteristics
Omitting conduit seals where the boundary crosses from classified to unclassified — common 501.15 violation
Using non-IS equipment in a Class I Div 1 location where the original design assumed an IS loop — IS calculation invalidated by component substitution
articles.divergence_notes: NEC uses two parallel classification systems — Division (legacy US, Arts 500-503) and Zone (IEC-aligned, Arts 505-506). IEC 60079 uses Zone only. NEC Group A/B/C/D (gas) and E/F/G (dust) map approximately to IEC IIA/IIB/IIC and IIIA/IIIB/IIIC but with material-specific differences. See hazardous-locations-classification.json for the comprehensive conversion matrix.
articles.usage_notes: On a new design, ask the AHJ early which classification system is preferred — many jurisdictions accept either, but mixing systems within one project is problematic.
